name: Grok 4.3
description: xAI's current flagship model released in early May 2026, with a 1M token context window, reasoning, function calling, and structured outputs at aggressive pricing ($1.25/$2.50 per 1M tokens). Strong frontier performance, but a thinner enterprise compliance posture than Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google.

evidence.value: Frontier coding performance positioned as flagship successor to Grok 4.1
Competitive with frontier peers on agentic coding evaluations

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Review of provider documentation and third-party benchmark aggregators

evidence.value: Native reasoning mode with strong math and science performance

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Review of reasoning benchmark results from provider and aggregators

evidence.value: Top-tier placement among frontier models in crowdsourced comparisons
High usage and quality ratings since launch

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Crowdsourced arena comparisons and aggregator quality metrics

evidence.value: Structured outputs and function calling support deterministic integration patterns

output_consistency.methodology: Review of structured output features and community reports of repeated-prompt behavior

evidence.value: Typical time-to-full-response around 2s for standard prompts (non-reasoning mode)

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency from third-party API benchmarking

evidence.value: Tail latency higher when extended reasoning is engaged

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time from third-party benchmarking; reasoning mode adds variance

evidence.value: 1M token context window; higher per-token rate applies above 200K tokens
Generally stable availability since launch with occasional incidents

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Frontier-class performance with a 1M context window and reasoning, function calling, and structured outputs. Release date sources conflict (2026-04-30 per OpenRouter vs 2026-05-06 per llm-stats); xAI documentation is treated as primary.

evidence.value: Hardened system prompt handling; limited published red-team data

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection patterns and review of published safety material

evidence.value: Safety improvements cited at launch; less third-party adversarial testing than peers

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Review of adversarial prompt testing results and community jailbreak reports

evidence.value: API data handling documented; fewer contractual controls than major enterprise providers

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling commitments

evidence.value: Content moderation in place; xAI publishes less safety evaluation detail than Anthropic/OpenAI/Google

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories and review of published evaluations

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting, team management in console

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and authentication mechanisms

security.notes: Reasonable baseline security, but xAI publishes substantially less safety and red-team documentation than Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google.

evidence.value: US-based infrastructure; no published regional residency options

data_residency.methodology: Review of provider documentation and enterprise materials

evidence.value: API customer data not used for training by default per policy

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

evidence.value: Limited retention for abuse monitoring; zero-retention requires enterprise agreement

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and data retention policies

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; no built-in PII tooling

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II; thinner certification portfolio (no HIPAA BAA program, limited GDPR tooling) vs Anthropic/OpenAI/Google

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications against major enterprise provider baselines

evidence.value: Zero-data-retention available only via negotiated enterprise terms

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of data handling practices and enterprise contract options

privacy_compliance.notes: xAI's enterprise compliance posture remains thinner than Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google: SOC 2 in place but no HIPAA eligibility program and fewer regulated-industry attestations.

evidence.value: Reasoning traces available via API for inspection

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: Continued hallucination reductions claimed at launch, building on Grok 4.1 improvements

hallucination_rate.methodology: Review of provider claims and factual QA testing

evidence.value: Limited published bias evaluation; past Grok versions drew scrutiny over politically tuned behavior

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of bias benchmark disclosures and independent reporting

evidence.value: Reasoning mode expresses uncertainty reasonably well

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Detailed model page with capabilities, pricing, limits, and feature support

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General description including X platform data; detailed sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Built-in moderation with developer controls; lighter-touch defaults than peers

trust_transparency.notes: Good developer-facing documentation and inspectable reasoning, but less published safety/bias evaluation than major competitors.

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible API with reasoning, function calling, structured outputs, and prompt caching

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs plus broad compatibility with OpenAI client libraries

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Retired Grok model slugs silently redirect to grok-4.3 rather than returning errors

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of deprecation/migration practices; silent redirects of retired slugs reduce predictability for pinned workloads
versioning_policy.notes: Silent redirection of retired model slugs to grok-4.3 can change behavior of production systems without explicit failure signals

evidence.value: Usage dashboard with spend and rate limit visibility

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Improving documentation; support channels lighter than major cloud providers

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available via OpenRouter and major LLM frameworks; growing third-party adoption

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations and tools

evidence.value: Clear commercial API terms; enterprise agreements available

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Strong API and pricing, but the May 2026 retirement wave (with silent slug redirects to grok-4.3) highlights an aggressive deprecation culture enterprises should plan around.

code-generation.notes: Frontier-class coding with function calling and structured outputs at very competitive pricing.

customer-support.notes: Fast, capable, and cheap for support workloads; compliance posture may limit regulated deployments.

content-creation.notes: Strong long-form generation with current-events awareness from the X ecosystem.

data-analysis.notes: Strong reasoning over large inputs; 1M context handles big datasets, with higher per-token rates above 200K.

research-assistant.notes: 1M context plus reasoning makes it well suited to literature-scale synthesis at low cost.

legal-compliance.notes: Capable analytically, but thinner compliance certifications than Anthropic/OpenAI/Google providers.

healthcare.notes: No HIPAA eligibility program; not recommended for PHI workloads.

financial-analysis.notes: Strong quantitative reasoning and real-time information; verify compliance requirements first.

education.notes: Strong explanations at low cost; content controls are lighter-touch than peers.

creative-writing.notes: Distinctive voice and strong creative range; fewer content restrictions than competitors.

strengths: Aggressive pricing: $1.25/$2.50 per 1M tokens with $0.20 cached input
Full agentic feature set: reasoning, function calling, structured outputs
Frontier-class performance across coding, reasoning, and general tasks
limitations: Thinner enterprise compliance posture than Anthropic, OpenAI, or Google (no HIPAA program)
Retired Grok model slugs silently redirect to grok-4.3, risking unannounced behavior changes
Higher per-token rate applies above 200K context
Limited published safety, bias, and red-team evaluation detail
Zero-data-retention only via negotiated enterprise terms
Conflicting release-date records across aggregators reflect lighter release documentation
best_for: Cost-sensitive agentic and coding workloads needing frontier quality
Long-context analysis and research up to 1M tokens
Teams already on OpenAI-compatible tooling seeking cheaper frontier capacity
not_recommended_for: Healthcare workloads involving PHI (no HIPAA eligibility)
Regulated industries requiring deep compliance attestations
Production systems that cannot tolerate silent model-slug redirection
Organizations requiring contractual zero data retention by default

pricing.notes: Cached input $0.20 per 1M tokens. Higher per-token rate applies for requests above 200K context.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with native reasoning, function calling, and structured outputs
metadata.release_date: Early May 2026 (2026-04-30 per OpenRouter; 2026-05-06 per llm-stats)
